chapter1.title: What is Psychology
definitions: Psychology is the scientific study of behaviour and mental processes.

chapter2.title: Methods of Enquiry in Psychology
definitions: Methods of enquiry in psychology are systematic procedures used to study behaviour and mental processes.
questions: What are the ethical issues in psychological research?
chapter2.diagram: Diagram showing control and experimental group

chapter3.title: Human Development
definitions: Human development refers to the pattern of change from conception to old age.
questions: Describe Erikson’s theory of psychosocial development.
chapter3.diagram: Table showing stages of human development

chapter4.title: Learning
definitions: Learning is a relatively permanent change in behaviour as a result of experience.
questions: Differentiate between classical and operant conditioning.

chapter5.title: Human Memory
definitions: Memory is the ability to retain and recall information over time.

chapter6.title: Thinking
definitions: Thinking is a mental process involving manipulation of information to solve problems.

chapter7.title: Motivation and Emotion
definitions: Motivation is the process that initiates, guides and sustains goal-directed behaviour.

chapter8.title: Personality
definitions: Personality refers to unique and relatively stable patterns of behaviour, thoughts and emotions.
